description: Lock Scientific Correspondence Networks to Royal Society correspondence management and journal communication, not printed errata as the direct mechanism.

checks.reason: Scientific societies supplied the institutional organizer for correspondence, meetings, discussion, and publication.
Societies organize and enable the network without being identical to the communications.
Philosophical Transactions supplied the source-backed printed communication platform.
Scientific journals are the shared publication platform, not the whole correspondence network.
The Royal Society's first meeting followed a Gresham College astronomy lecture.
Public lecture culture is historical lineage for the Society's meeting/correspondence network.
Printed errata should not be restored unless the node is narrowed to correction publication rather than correspondence.
The node remains anchored to the 1665 Philosophical Transactions platform while noting earlier correspondence management.
